Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log with three cases The activities are RA Receive Application VF Validate Form AE Assess Eligibility RMI Request Missing Info AD Approval Decision SC Send Confirmation AC Archive Case Observing the cases Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We see that the main sequence is RA VF AE then optionally a loop that involves RMI and then VF again then AD SC AC

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In case 1 there is one RMI and then VF again In case 2 there is no RMI In case 3 there are two RMIs in a row and then VF again Note that after the loop if it occurs we always have AD SC AC

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: So we can think of the process as 1 Start with RA 2 Then VF 3 Then AE 4 Then optionally a loop that involves RMI and then VF again But note that in the loop we might have multiple RMIs Actually in the given log we see that after the first RMI we go to VF However in case 3 we have two RMIs in a row without VF in between This suggests that the loop might be after AE we can have RMI and then we go back to VF But in case 3 we have two RMIs in a row How can that happen

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Looking at the timestamps in case 3 AE at 0910 RMI at 0925 RMI at 0945 VF at 1020 It seems that after the first RMI we might have another RMI without going through VF Or maybe the loop is after AE we can have a sequence of RMIs one or more and then VF But note that in the given process tree operators we dont have a direct way to express one or more RMIs without using a loop

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the behavior is after AE we may have a sequence of one or more RMIs and then we must have VF again before moving to AD

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We can use a loop that does RMI and then either exits or does another RMI Then after the loop we do VF But then what about case 2 In case 2 we dont have any RMI So we dont enter the loop at all So we need to have a choice either skip the loop and go directly to AD or enter the loop which is RMI one or more times and then do VF and then AD

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: After AE we have an XOR between two branches Branch 1 AD SC AC Branch 2 loop RMI tau VF AD SC AC The loop RMI tau means do RMI then either exit or do tau and then RMI again This allows for one or more RMIs

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets check with the cases Case 1 RA VF AE choose branch2 RMI exit loop VF AD SC AC Case 2 RA VF AE choose branch1 AD SC AC Case 3 RA VF AE choose branch2 RMI tau RMI exit VF AD SC AC This matches

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: We can also think of a loop after AE as loop RMI VF tau but the loop operator always executes first child at least once so doesnt work for case 2 Therefore the XOR model fits
